Home › PDF tools › Merge PDF

Merge PDF files into one

Combine multiple PDFs, in the order you choose.

Files stay on your device. No accounts. Free.

Loading tool...

About this tool

Combine contracts, scans, chapters or statements into a single PDF. Files merge in the order listed, use the arrows next to each file to reorder before you merge.

Merging runs on pdf-lib inside your browser, which matters for exactly the kind of documents people merge: agreements, IDs, financial statements. There is no server in this pipeline, so they cannot leak.

How it works

  1. Drop your file(s), they are read locally on your device.
  2. Set the options you need and run the tool.
  3. Download the finished PDF, images or text.

Frequently asked questions

Is it safe to merge confidential documents here?

Yes, and this is the tool's whole reason to exist. The PDFs are read and combined by your own browser using pdf-lib; they are never transmitted, so there is no server copy to leak or get breached.

Can I control the order of the merged pages?

Yes. Files merge top to bottom in the order shown in the list, and each file has arrows to move it up or down before you press merge.

Is there a limit on file size or number of PDFs?

No fixed limit. The practical ceiling is your device's memory, and normal documents, even hundreds of pages, are far below it.

Related tools

  • Split PDF - Extract pages or ranges into separate PDFs.
  • Compress PDF - Shrink heavy PDFs to a fraction of their size.
  • Images to PDF - Combine JPG, PNG or WebP photos into one A4 PDF.
